data.title: Tanger Board Chair to retire, Luis Ubiñas appointed as successor
data.description:
Tanger (SKT) announced on Thursday the retirement of Board Chair Steven Tanger, and the appointment of Luis Ubiñas as a successor.

Steven Tanger is set to assume the title of Chair Emeritus.

Ubiñas currently serves as an independent director.

Additionally, Bridget Ryan-Berman will conclude her service as lead independent director and continue to serve on the board.

data.title: PetroTal Corp. reports Q4 results
data.description:
• PetroTal Corp. press release (TAL:CA): Q4

  • Average Q4 2025 sales and production of 15,059 and 15,258 barrels of oil per day, respectively.

  • Generated Adjusted EBITDA of $18.5M ($13.38/bbl) in Q4 2025 

  • PetroTal ended Q4 2025 with a total cash position of $139.1 million, of which $112.4 million was unrestricted.

data.title: Ceres Power Holdings Non-GAAP EPS of £24.52, revenue of £32.6M
data.description:
• Ceres Power Holdings (CPWHF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of £24.52.
  • Revenue of £32.6M (-37.2% Y/Y).
  • Gross profit of £22.7 million (2024: £40.2 million), maintaining sector-leading gross margin of 70% (2024: 77%).
  • The Group posted a loss of £47.5 million (2024: £28.3 million) for the period.
  • Adjusted EBITDA loss for 2025 increased to £32.5 million (2024: £22.3 million).
  • The Group ends the financial year in a strong position with £83.3 million in cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments (2024: £102.5 million).

data.title: Coinbase, Better Home & Finance launch crypto-backed mortgages
data.description:
Coinbase Global (COIN) and Better Home & Finance Holding (BETR) on Thursday announced the launch of token-backed mortgages.

Token-backed mortgages, which will be originated and serviced by Better Home & Finance, are set to benefit from the same backing of Fannie Mae (FNMA) as other conforming mortgages. Bitcoin (BTC-USD) and USDC (USDC-USD) pledges are powered by Coinbase.

The product enables Americans who qualify for a mortgage with BETR to pledge Bitcoin or Circle Internet Group's (CRCL) USDC as collateral to fund their cash down payment.

The benefits include if BTC drops in value, the mortgage terms remain unchanged, and no additional collateral is required.

Token-backed mortgages let borrowers secure standard conforming mortgages without liquidating tokenized assets or potentially triggering a taxable event.

"Better was founded to make homeownership more accessible for all Americans, and this partnership with Coinbase introduces a new pathway to realizing the American Dream for the 52 million Americans who own digital assets," said BETR CEO Vishal Garg.

data.title: Analog Devices, Microchip may still outperform even as Cantor sees analog chips rebound
data.description:
Analog Devices (ADI) and Microchip (MCHP) may still provide outperformance even as the analog chip market rebounds, Cantor Fitzgerald said.

“The overarching takeaway ... optimism in the cyclical recovery is rising,” Cantor Fitzgerald analysts wrote in a note to clients. “Customer orders are trending positively. Lead times are extending in spots. And the pricing backdrop is favorable. So, while this has been an industry marred with head fakes and 2H optimism for 2-3 [years] now (leading to meaningful underperformance for the group vs. the SOX over the same timeframe), signals are seemingly turning more positive and there is a view that we are now finally on the other side of the cycle.”

Ahead of the first-quarter results. Cantor Fitzgerald analysts said there is the potential for Analog Devices and Microchip to outperform.

“For ADI, we expect a slight beat/raise, with focus on outsized AI-related/ADEF exposure supporting continued upside for shares,” the analysts explained. “For MCHP, we see solid upside to numbers with [management] bulled up on both cyclical and structural dynamics – also, greater end-market transparency next [quarter] could be a sneaky catalyst.”

For Texas Instruments (TXN), the analysts don't expect a repeat of last quarter's strong results, while they said NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) is the firm's favorite value name. 

Conversely, the firm said there is “downside risk” to Onsemi (ON), citing several business exits and overexposure to the automotive market.

data.title: 22nd Century outlines strategy to double store count and launch Pinnacle Pure in 2026 while driving VLN market expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: 22nd Century Group (XXII) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Lawrence Firestone presented 2025 as a transition year, emphasizing the closure of legacy business issues, reduction in manufacturing overhead, and the payoff of all structured debt. He highlighted the launch of new brands and products, stating, “We are now in the market with our VLN combustible cigarette products and smoking consumers are finding our products in 1,636 retail outlets in 23 states.”
  • Firestone indicated a strategic focus on expanding distribution and forming partnerships, noting, “We welcome other brands to join the battle and introduce a VLN concept within their own brands… they could license our VLN brand and use it within their brand lineup.”
  • Firestone described regulatory advances, including the MRTP renewal application filed with the FDA in December 2025, and discussed ongoing international dialogues.
  • Looking ahead, Firestone announced, “We now have over $10 million in cash on the balance sheet to implement our strategy” and outlined plans to add distribution points in the Northeast, Mid-Atlantic, and California, as well as the launch of Pinnacle Pure, a new tobacco and water style cigarette.
  • CFO Daniel Otto reported, “During the year, we exited several unprofitable high-volume revenue streams and focused our resources on building the foundation for growth in our reduced nicotine VLN cigarette portfolio and partner VLN brands.”
  • Otto added, “In the fourth quarter of 2025, we shipped approximately 8,800 cartons of newly branded VLN and partner VLN to our customers… These products remain central to our strategy as they typically generate gross profit margin in the range of 20% to 30%.”

Outlook

  • Management projects revenue to remain consistent in Q1 2026 before sequential growth as additional distribution points are added. Otto stated, “We estimate increasing current store count by more than double by the end of 2026.”
  • Firestone referenced the plan to expand marketing and tracking rate of sale metrics, as well as increasing headcount to support launches and marketing initiatives.
  • The company intends to launch Pinnacle Pure and expand VLN under three brands, with new marketing collateral and support aimed at driving rate of sale.

Financial Results

  • Otto reported net revenue of approximately $3.6 million for Q4 2025, compared to $4 million in Q3 2025, and shipment of approximately 248,000 cartons versus 517,000 cartons in the prior quarter. The decline reflected a strategic move away from lower-margin contracts.
  • Gross loss for Q4 improved to approximately $0.8 million from $1.1 million in Q3, indicating early benefits from cost reduction and restructuring efforts.
  • Operating loss was $2.8 million, down from $3.2 million in Q3, while adjusted EBITDA for the quarter was a loss of $2.4 million compared with a $2.9 million loss in Q3.
  • Year-end cash and cash equivalents totaled $7.1 million with zero long-term debt, and a subsequent capital raise brought the cash position to over $10 million.
  • Inventory increased to approximately $4.3 million, reflecting preparation for expanded VLN production.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: The current transcript did not include a Q&A section with analyst questions or management responses.

Sentiment Analysis

  • There were no analyst questions in the current quarter, so no analyst sentiment is discernible from the transcript.
  • Management maintained a positive and confident tone throughout the prepared remarks, with Firestone stating, “We are up for the challenge. And as we've seen, we have consumers who are up for a change.” The tone was forward-looking and focused on growth, contrasting with the previous quarter’s more cautious optimism during the restructuring phase.
  • Compared to Q3, management’s tone in Q4 was more assertive about entering a growth phase, while the previous quarter’s commentary was more reflective on restructuring and balance sheet improvements.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company moved from a restructuring and cleanup focus in Q3 2025 to active expansion and growth in Q4 2025.
  • Key changes include the closure of unprofitable contracts, the exit from lower-margin business lines, and the launch of new branded VLN products and Pinnacle Pure.
  • The store count with VLN products increased from approximately 1,500 stores in 21 states to 1,636 stores in 23 states.
  • Management’s confidence increased in Q4, with more emphasis on new product launches, market expansion, and capital availability for growth initiatives.
  • No analyst Q&A in Q4, while Q3 included questions on cash usage, warrants, and breakeven timelines.

Risks and Concerns

  • Firestone acknowledged the competitive landscape, noting that “none of our competitors are going to move over and let us in,” and highlighted the need to win on every front to gain retail positions.
  • Regulatory uncertainty remains, with the FDA’s low nicotine proposed rule under internal review. Firestone stated, “The forces of politics, financial power and taxes all cast aside the care and the cost of care and the mortality of our smoking population who are addicted.”
  • International regulatory adoption is at an early stage, with ongoing dialogues but no immediate impact.
  • Otto indicated that initial sales data is limited and that “rate of sale metrics” would be tracked in 2026 to monitor product adoption.

Final Takeaway

22nd Century Group’s leadership emphasized that 2025 marked the transition from restructuring to growth, setting the stage for aggressive expansion in 2026. With a debt-free balance sheet, more than $10 million in cash, and a sharpened strategic focus on proprietary VLN products and new launches such as Pinnacle Pure, the company aims to double its retail store count, drive sequential revenue growth, and leverage its regulatory positioning in the evolving tobacco market. Management’s tone was confident and growth-oriented, signaling that the foundation is set for the next phase of business development, with a keen eye on increasing distribution, product adoption, and profitability in the coming year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Wall Street trades lower as attention remains focused on the Middle East conflict
data.description:
Wall Street edged lower on Thursday as investors kept a close eye on escalating U.S.-Iran tensions after Tehran dismissed a U.S. proposal aimed at ending the conflict.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was last at -0.1%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.5%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.8%. 

From a sector-by-sector standpoint, four of the 11 S&P segments were in the red, with communication services at the bottom. At the other end, consumer staples has been the strongest performer of the session. 

Treasury yields rose across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) climbed 2 basis points to 4.36%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) added 4 basis points to 3.93%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) edged up 1 basis point to 4.92%. 

A senior political-security official has reportedly told Iranian news network Press TV that Iran has formally rejected the American proposal, insisting that Tehran will dictate the terms and timing of the war’s conclusion. 

On the economic front, initial jobless claims data for the week ended March 21 rose by 5K to 210K, against the consensus of 210K. 

As for stocks that were on the move, Best Buy (BBY) climbed +8.7%, and shares of Applovin Corporation (APP) fell -8.6%.

data.title: Adobe downgraded by William Blair on 'intense competition'
data.description:
Adobe (ADBE) was downgraded by William Blair on Thursday, as the research firm cited “intense competition” in the creative market.

“While we acknowledge that shares are inexpensive at 9 times free cash flow, our primary concern is around the intense competition Adobe faces, particularly in core Creative Cloud,” analyst Arjun Bhatia wrote in a note to clients. “The competitive environment raises legitimate questions about what the future looks like for Adobe including what it means for pricing dynamics/pricing power, long-term economics, ability to differentiate, right to win the AI opportunity, the role of professional creators, and Adobe’s hefty margin profile. To be clear, we are not calling Adobe an 'AI loser' with this downgrade but acknowledging that the unknown answers to these questions are likely to keep the stock at least range-bound until there is more clarity.”

Bhatia, who lowered the rating on Adobe to Market Perform from Outperform, added artificial intelligence has taken the creative market “by storm” and “essentially overnight, democratized the highly technical skills creative professionals had built.”

He pointed to the fact that Canva and Figma (FIG) have started to gain traction on the edges of Adobe's core market of creative professionals. Canva has hit the low end, and Figma has gone after the user interface and user experience design market. And while Adobe's Digital Media segment is at a $19B revenue run rate, Canva is now at $4B and growing at more than 30%, while Figma is at $1.2B and growing at 40%.

Other companies, such as Midjourney, Synthesia, Runway, and StabilityAI have also entered the market, putting further pressure on Adobe, Bhatia added, not to mention the various pushes from Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), OpenAI (OPENAI), and Apple (AAPL).

“All of this points to a competitive environment that is not just intense, but also seems to be intensifying. It is early enough in the AI cycle that winners and losers are hard to discern,” Bhatia added. “However, several key questions related to Adobe’s future are unlikely to be resolved in the near term.”

data.title: Nebius launches AI Cloud 3.5 featuring serverless AI to speed up development times
data.description:
Nebius (NBIS) has launched AI Cloud 3.5, which adds several new features to its full-stack cloud platform, including serverless artificial intelligence computing.

"The introduction of serverless features gives developers the ability to launch workloads almost instantly, eliminating the need for AI teams to spend significant time configuring infrastructure before they can run experiments, train, or serve models in production," Nebius said. "Infrastructure configuration and runtime management are handled by the Nebius platform, enabling developers to focus on building applications instead of managing environments."

The Netherlands-based AI cloud company also expanded its GPU offerings with Nvidia's (NVDA) RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition, which can handle workloads involving AI inference, industrial robotics, physical AI simulations, visual computing, and drug discovery.

Version 3.5 of Nebius AI Cloud Aether also introduces Data Transfer Service, which reduces data management overhead for teams working across environments by simplifying data migration and replication between external storage systems and Nebius cloud regions.

The new serverless feature is currently available in public preview.
